project.creator: Bang
environment: 1. Frontend: React hearing-room verdict panel and hearings API
2. Backend: NestJS disputes controller, hearing verdict orchestrator, and verdict service
3. Database: PostgreSQL via TypeORM dispute, hearing, escrow, wallet, and ledger entities
project.notes: Strict feature-based workbook for UC-78 Staff Dispute Resolution. Result values are based on the local Jest execution completed on 23/03/2026 for the hearing-room verdict orchestration flow and the controller redirect behavior.

changeLog.description: Initial feature/function-based workbook generated from the UC-78 specification, hearing-room UI/backend implementation, and executed Jest evidence.
changeLog.reference: UC-78 use-case specification and dispute hearing verdict specs

features.name: Staff Dispute Resolution
features.testRequirement: Validate the UC-78 hearing-room verdict flow, including hearing endpoint delegation, moderator/admin access control, readiness checklist enforcement, mandatory verdict reasoning and minutes, money split computation, downstream failure safeguards, and the blocked legacy resolve endpoint.

functions.name: Issue Verdict From Hearing Room
functions.description: Submit the final dispute verdict from the hearing-room endpoint and complete the hearing-close workflow.
functions.preCondition: A dispute hearing exists, the caller is authenticated as staff or admin, and the verdict payload includes hearing minutes and verdict details.

functions.name: Enforce Access and Hearing-State Guards
functions.description: Block verdict issuance when the caller lacks moderator/admin authority or when the hearing cannot legally accept a verdict yet.
functions.preCondition: A hearing exists; negative cases intentionally violate actor, status, or readiness requirements.

functions.name: Validate Verdict Reasoning and Minutes
functions.description: Require the mandatory reasoning fields, verdict result, and hearing minutes before a final verdict is accepted.
functions.preCondition: The hearing-room form is open and the caller is authorized to issue verdicts.

functions.name: Compute and Validate Money Distribution
functions.description: Translate split ratio or explicit payout amounts into escrow distribution outputs and reject invalid financial states.
functions.preCondition: The dispute is linked to an escrow-backed milestone and the verdict payload includes either split ratio or explicit payout amounts.

functions.name: Handle Resolution Failure Safeguards
functions.description: Return a conflict response when verdict issuance succeeds but hearing close-out fails afterward.
functions.preCondition: Verdict issuance reaches the backend and the hearing auto-close step fails downstream.

functions.name: Block Legacy Resolve Endpoint
functions.description: Prevent direct use of the deprecated dispute resolve endpoint and redirect callers to the hearing-room flow.
functions.preCondition: A caller attempts to use POST /disputes/:id/resolve instead of the hearing verdict endpoint.

testCases.description: Delegate hearing-room verdict issuance from the controller to the orchestrator and return a success envelope.
testCases.procedure: 1. Authenticate as a staff user.\n2. Call POST /disputes/hearings/{hearingId}/verdict with verdict and closeHearing payloads.\n3. Observe the controller call into the orchestrator and inspect the returned response.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The controller forwards hearingId, requester id, requester role, and dto to HearingVerdictOrchestratorService.issueHearingVerdict.\n2. The controller returns success = true with message 'Verdict issued and hearing closed'.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: the controller delegated hearing-1, staff-1, UserRole.STAFF, and the payload to HearingVerdictOrchestratorService.issueHearingVerdict, then returned { success: true, message: 'Verdict issued and hearing closed', data: { verdict: { id: 'verdict-1' } } }.
testCases.dependencies: A valid hearing id, authenticated staff session, and a well-formed IssueHearingVerdictDto payload.
testCases.note: Evidence: disputes.controller.spec.ts -> delegates hearing-room verdict issuance to the orchestrator.

testCases.description: Allow an admin to issue a hearing verdict even when that admin is not the assigned moderator.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare an active hearing whose moderator is a different staff user.\n2. Authenticate as an admin.\n3. Submit a hearing verdict payload from the hearing-room flow.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Admin access bypasses the assigned-moderator restriction.\n2. The orchestrator delegates the verdict to VerdictService.issueVerdict.\n3. Verdict issuance proceeds with the admin id and role.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: issueHearingVerdict accepted requester admin-1 with UserRole.ADMIN even though hearing.moderatorId was staff-1, and it delegated a SPLIT verdict for dispute-1 to VerdictService.issueVerdict.
testCases.dependencies: Active hearing, authenticated admin user, and valid verdict + closeHearing payload.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> allows admins to issue a hearing verdict even when they are not the assigned moderator.

testCases.description: Issue a split verdict successfully from Hearing Room, compute the split, close the hearing, and return transfer summary data.
testCases.procedure: 1. Open an IN_PROGRESS hearing as the assigned moderator.\n2. Submit a SPLIT verdict with reasoning, admin comment, splitRatioClient = 40, and closeHearing minutes.\n3. Observe the verdict issuance and hearing close response.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The service computes amountToClient = 40 and amountToFreelancer = 60 from the escrow remainder.
2. VerdictService.issueVerdict is called with the computed values.
3. HearingService.endHearing is called with summary and findings.
4. The response contains verdict, ended hearing data, and transfer summary.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: for fundedAmount 120 and platformFee 20, the orchestrator computed amountToClient 40 and amountToFreelancer 60, called VerdictService.issueVerdict and HearingService.endHearing, and returned verdict id verdict-1, hearing status ENDED, and transferSummary.transferCount = 2.
testCases.dependencies: Assigned moderator session, IN_PROGRESS hearing, dispute-linked escrow, and complete verdict reasoning plus hearing minutes.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> computes split amounts from splitRatioClient and delegates verdict issuance and hearing close.

testCases.description: Forbid a staff user who is not the assigned moderator from issuing the final verdict.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare an active hearing moderated by staff-1.\n2. Authenticate as staff-2.\n3. Attempt to issue the hearing verdict.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The orchestrator checks moderator authority.\n2. The request is rejected with ForbiddenException.\n3. Verdict issuance does not proceed.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: issueHearingVerdict rejected requester staff-2 with ForbiddenException before verdict issuance continued.
testCases.dependencies: Active hearing, authenticated staff user who is not the assigned moderator, and verdict payload.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> forbids staff who is not the assigned moderator from issuing a verdict.

testCases.description: Reject verdict issuance when the hearing is not active.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare a hearing in SCHEDULED status.\n2. Authenticate as the assigned moderator.\n3. Attempt to issue the final verdict.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The orchestrator validates the hearing status.\n2. A ConflictException is raised with hearing-not-active semantics.\n3. No verdict is issued.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: when hearing.status was SCHEDULED, issueHearingVerdict threw ConflictException and blocked verdict issuance.
testCases.dependencies: Existing hearing not in IN_PROGRESS or PAUSED status and authenticated assigned moderator.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> rejects verdict issuance when the hearing is not active.

testCases.description: Reject verdict issuance when the hearing readiness checklist is not satisfied.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare an otherwise valid hearing verdict submission.\n2. Configure verdict readiness with canIssueVerdict = false and unmet checklist items.\n3. Attempt to issue the verdict.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The readiness checklist is evaluated before verdict issuance.\n2. A ConflictException is returned with checklist context.\n3. Verdict issuance stops until missing items are resolved.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: issueHearingVerdict threw ConflictException when evaluateHearingReadiness returned canIssueVerdict = false with unmet checklist entries such as minutes-ready.
testCases.dependencies: Active hearing, authorized requester, and readiness service returning unmet checklist items.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> rejects verdict issuance when the hearing readiness checklist is unmet.

testCases.description: Require both summary and findings before the final verdict can be issued.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare a valid verdict payload.\n2. Clear the closeHearing summary or findings field.\n3. Submit the verdict from the hearing-room flow.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The backend validates hearing minutes before issuing the verdict.\n2. A BadRequestException is returned.\n3. The final verdict is not issued.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: with summary set to whitespace, issueHearingVerdict threw BadRequestException and blocked final verdict issuance.
testCases.dependencies: Authorized requester and otherwise valid verdict payload except missing hearing-minute content.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> requires summary and findings before issuing the final verdict.

testCases.description: Require a no-show note when one or more required hearing participants are absent.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare a valid verdict submission.\n2. Return absentRequiredParticipants from the readiness service.\n3. Submit without closeHearing.noShowNote.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The backend detects missing no-show documentation.\n2. A BadRequestException is returned.\n3. The verdict is not issued until the note is supplied.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: when readiness.absentRequiredParticipants contained client-1 and noShowNote was missing, issueHearingVerdict raised BadRequestException.
testCases.dependencies: Active hearing, authorized requester, and readiness service indicating at least one required participant is absent.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> requires a no-show note when required participants are absent.

testCases.description: Require at least one violated policy in the verdict reasoning payload.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare a valid verdict submission.\n2. Clear verdict.reasoning.violatedPolicies so the list is empty.\n3. Submit the hearing verdict.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The backend validates violatedPolicies before issuing the verdict.\n2. A BadRequestException is returned.\n3. Verdict issuance is blocked.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: when verdict.reasoning.violatedPolicies was [], issueHearingVerdict threw BadRequestException and prevented verdict issuance.
testCases.dependencies: Authorized requester and otherwise valid verdict payload except missing violated policy data.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> requires at least one violated policy in the verdict reasoning.

testCases.description: Require an explicit verdict result before a final decision can be issued.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare a verdict payload.\n2. Remove verdict.result and verdict.verdict.\n3. Submit the hearing verdict.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The backend checks for the required result field.\n2. A BadRequestException is returned.\n3. No verdict is issued.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: after removing both result fields, issueHearingVerdict threw BadRequestException because the verdict result was missing.
testCases.dependencies: Authorized requester and otherwise valid payload except for missing result selection.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> requires a verdict result before issuing the final decision.

testCases.description: Use explicitly provided payout amounts instead of recomputing the split from splitRatioClient.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare a SPLIT verdict with amountToClient = 15, amountToFreelancer = 85, and splitRatioClient = 40.\n2. Submit the hearing verdict as the assigned moderator.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The orchestrator preserves the explicit payout amounts.\n2. It passes amountToClient = 15 and amountToFreelancer = 85 to VerdictService.issueVerdict.\n3. The split ratio is not used to recompute the payout values.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: issueHearingVerdict delegated amountToClient 15 and amountToFreelancer 85 to VerdictService.issueVerdict even though splitRatioClient was also present.
testCases.dependencies: Assigned moderator session, valid SPLIT verdict payload, and dispute-linked escrow.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> uses explicitly provided payout amounts instead of recomputing the split.

testCases.description: Support the lower split boundary where the client receives 0% and the freelancer receives 100% of the post-fee remainder.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare a SPLIT verdict with splitRatioClient = 0.\n2. Submit the hearing verdict as the assigned moderator.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The orchestrator computes amountToClient = 0.\n2. The remaining post-fee amount is assigned to the freelancer.\n3. VerdictService.issueVerdict receives the boundary payout values.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: with splitRatioClient = 0, issueHearingVerdict delegated amountToClient 0 and amountToFreelancer 100 to VerdictService.issueVerdict.
testCases.dependencies: Assigned moderator session, valid SPLIT verdict payload, and dispute-linked escrow with non-negative post-fee remainder.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> supports the lower split boundary when splitRatioClient is 0.

testCases.description: Support the upper split boundary where the client receives 100% and the freelancer receives 0% of the post-fee remainder.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare a SPLIT verdict with splitRatioClient = 100.\n2. Submit the hearing verdict as the assigned moderator.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The orchestrator computes amountToClient = 100% of the post-fee remainder.\n2. amountToFreelancer becomes 0.\n3. VerdictService.issueVerdict receives the boundary payout values.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: with splitRatioClient = 100, issueHearingVerdict delegated amountToClient 100 and amountToFreelancer 0 to VerdictService.issueVerdict.
testCases.dependencies: Assigned moderator session, valid SPLIT verdict payload, and dispute-linked escrow with non-negative post-fee remainder.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> supports the upper split boundary when splitRatioClient is 100.

testCases.description: Reject verdict issuance when the escrow record for the dispute milestone cannot be found.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare an otherwise valid hearing verdict submission.\n2. Remove the escrow record lookup result for the dispute milestone.\n3. Submit the verdict.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The backend looks up the milestone escrow before computing payouts.\n2. A not-found error is returned.\n3. No verdict is issued.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: when escrowRepo.findOne returned null, issueHearingVerdict failed with 'Escrow for milestone milestone-1 not found'.
testCases.dependencies: Authorized requester and hearing verdict payload, with the target milestone lacking an escrow record.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> rejects verdict issuance when the escrow record for the hearing milestone is missing.

testCases.description: Reject verdict issuance when the platform fee exceeds the funded escrow amount.
testCases.procedure: 1. Prepare an otherwise valid hearing verdict submission.\n2. Set fundedAmount lower than platformFee on the escrow record.\n3. Submit the verdict.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The orchestrator computes the post-fee remainder.\n2. The backend detects the invalid financial state.\n3. Verdict issuance is rejected before money distribution proceeds.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: with fundedAmount 50 and platformFee 60, issueHearingVerdict threw 'Platform fee exceeds funded amount'.
testCases.dependencies: Authorized requester, valid hearing verdict payload, and escrow data where platformFee is greater than fundedAmount.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> rejects verdict issuance when the platform fee exceeds the funded amount.

testCases.description: Surface a conflict response when verdict issuance succeeds but hearing auto-close fails afterward.
testCases.procedure:
1. Prepare a valid hearing verdict submission.
2. Allow VerdictService.issueVerdict to succeed.
3. Force HearingService.endHearing to fail.
4. Submit the verdict.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Verdict issuance completes first.\n2. The auto-close failure is transformed into a ConflictException with manual follow-up guidance.\n3. The response indicates that the verdict exists but hearing close-out must be reviewed manually.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: when HearingService.endHearing rejected with 'minutes persistence failed', issueHearingVerdict surfaced a ConflictException instead of silently succeeding.
testCases.dependencies: Authorized requester, valid verdict payload, successful verdict issuance, and a downstream hearing close failure.
testCases.note: Evidence: hearing-verdict-orchestrator.service.spec.ts -> surfaces a conflict when verdict issuance succeeds but hearing auto-close fails.

testCases.description: Block the deprecated POST /disputes/{id}/resolve endpoint and direct the caller to the hearing-room verdict route.
testCases.procedure: 1. Authenticate as staff.\n2. Call POST /disputes/{id}/resolve with a legacy verdict payload.\n3. Observe the controller response.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The legacy endpoint does not execute dispute resolution.\n2. A ConflictException is returned with code VERDICT_ONLY_IN_HEARING.\n3. The response instructs the caller to use POST /disputes/hearings/{hearingId}/verdict instead.
testCases.actualResults: Executed Jest case passed: controller.resolveDispute rejected with ConflictException { code: 'VERDICT_ONLY_IN_HEARING', message: 'Verdict can only be issued from Hearing Room.', action: 'Use POST /disputes/hearings/:hearingId/verdict.' }.
testCases.dependencies: Authenticated staff or admin session and a call to the deprecated /disputes/:id/resolve route.
testCases.note: Evidence: disputes.controller.spec.ts -> blocks the legacy resolve endpoint and directs callers to the hearing-room flow.
